audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Corinthians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for cleansing holiness, open heart, Macedonia pressure, Titus comfort, grief according to Divine, earnestness and restoration.

verses.restored: Therefore, having these promises, beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from every defilement of flesh and spirit, completing holiness in reverence/fear of the Divine.
Make room for us. We wronged no one, corrupted no one, exploited no one.
I do not say this for condemnation; for I have said before that you are in our hearts, to die together and live together.
Great is my boldness toward you; great is my boasting on your behalf. I have been filled with comfort/encouragement; I overflow with joy in all our pressure.
For even when we came into Macedonia, our flesh had no rest, but we were pressured in every way: outside conflicts, inside fears.
But the Divine, the One comforting/encouraging the lowly, comforted/encouraged us by the arrival of Titus,

and not only by his arrival, but also by the comfort/encouragement with which he was comforted/encouraged among you, reporting to us your longing, your mourning, your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced even more.
For even if I grieved you by the letter, I do not regret it; even if I did regret, for I see that the letter grieved you, though only for an hour,
now I rejoice, not that you were grieved, but that you were grieved into turning/realignment; for you were grieved according to the Divine, so that in nothing you might suffer loss from us.
For grief according to the Divine works turning/realignment into salvation/healing without regret; but the grief of the world works death.

For look at this very thing, that you were grieved according to the Divine: what earnestness it worked in you, but also defense, indignation, fear/reverence, longing, zeal, correction. In everything you showed yourselves to be pure in the matter.
So then, if I also wrote to you, it was not because of the one doing wrong, nor because of the one wronged, but so that your earnestness for us might be made manifest to you before the Divine.
Because of this we have been comforted/encouraged. And besides our comfort/encouragement, we rejoiced much more abundantly over the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by all of you.
For if I have boasted anything to him about you, I was not put to shame; but as we spoke all things to you in truth, so also our boasting before Titus became truth.
And his inward affections are more abundantly toward you, remembering the obedience of you all, how with fear/reverence and trembling you received him.
I rejoice that in everything I am courageous/confident in you.

translation_notes: Grief according to the Divine is transformative grief that leads to turning and life, not shame-collapse.
Paul distinguishes restorative grief from worldly grief that works death.
